LHX.sentence: L3Harris is up about 2% today and their order book keeps growing steadily, which is what you want to see from a defense contractor.

ASML.sentence: ASML gained over 2% as chip companies keep ordering their machines — they're the only company that makes the most advanced ones, so demand stays strong.

LIN.sentence: Linde is having a quiet day, which is normal for them — industrial gas demand is steady and they keep their profit margins healthy.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft is essentially flat, trading cheaper than Apple and Google right now even though their AI cloud business keeps growing.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher looks like a bargain after getting beaten down — they supply lab equipment to every biotech and pharma company, so when those industries recover, this one should too.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is steady as usual — pipeline companies are like toll roads, and this one keeps collecting fees while paying a solid dividend.

NOW.sentence: ServiceNow is up about 2% but still way down from its highs, which seems odd since big companies keep buying their AI workflow tools.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore is down slightly — they mine copper and trade commodities, so they're sensitive to any worry about global economic growth.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin dropped over 2% today and is trading well below its recent highs, which might be a buying opportunity if you believe in the long-term story.

KOF.sentence: Coca-Cola FEMSA is flat — the Mexican peso has been stable, which helps since they sell Coke across Latin America.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras is down a bit but oil prices are still way above what they need to be profitable and pay big dividends.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk dropped over 3% but is trading below what their ships are actually worth, which often signals the market is too pessimistic about global shipping.
